---
title: "run susieR"
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    code_folding: hide
    toc: true
date: "2026-02-02"
---


```{r, message=F, warning=F}
#install.packages("susieR")
rm(list=ls())
library(rprojroot)
library(susieR)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(susieR)
library(DT)
root <- rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file()
```


```{r, message=F, warning=F, eval=F}
######## load GWAS summary data ##########

#dfss_summary=read.table(file.path(root, "../data_samples/EUR_DFSS_incl_HCHSSOL.txt"), header=T)
#saveRDS(dfss_summary, file=file.path(root, "../data_samples/dfss_summary.RDS"))
dfss_summary=readRDS(file.path(root, "../data_samples/dfss_summary.RDS"))
head(dfss_summary)
dim(dfss_summary)
```





## DFSS

### prepare the input data 

```{r, message=F, warning=F}

############################
# Paths
############################
gwas_file=file.path(root, "../data_samples/dfss_summary.RDS")

ld_dir    <- file.path(root,"../LDMatrix/04_compute_LD_for_Shungin2019_EUR_DFSS_output")
#ld_dir    <- file.path(root,"../LDMatrix/04_compute_LD_for_Shungin2019_EUR_Nteeth_output")

WINDOW <- 500000
L_MAX  <- 10
```


```{r, message=F, warning=F}

############################
# Load GWAS
############################
gwas <- readRDS(gwas_file)
gwas$MarkerName <- sub(":ID$", "", gwas$MarkerName)  # remove "ID" if any 
gwas <- gwas %>% distinct(MarkerName, .keep_all = TRUE)  # remove duplicate MarkerName


table(grepl(":ID$", gwas$MarkerName)) # Quick sanity check
gwas <- as.data.table(gwas)

# Parse chr / pos from MarkerName
gwas[, c("CHR", "BP") := tstrsplit(MarkerName, ":", fixed = TRUE, keep = 1:2)]
gwas[, CHR := as.integer(CHR)]
gwas[, BP  := as.integer(BP)]


############################
# List LD files
############################
ld_files <- list.files(ld_dir, pattern = "\\.ld$", full.names = TRUE)

############################
# Container for results
############################
susie_results <- vector("list", length(ld_files))
names(susie_results) <- basename(ld_files)


```



### run susieR



```{r, message=F, warning=F, eval=F}
for (i in seq_along(ld_files)) 
{
#  i=1
  ############################
  # Load LD (Square Matrix)
  ############################
  ld_file <- ld_files[i]
  locus_id <- sub("^LD_|\\.ld$", "", basename(ld_file)) 
  cat("SNP:", locus_id, "is running", "\n")

  # Parse Chr and Pos from filename
  base <- sub("\\.ld$", "", locus_id) 
  chr <- as.integer(sub("_.*", "", base)) 
  pos <- as.integer(sub(".*_", "", base))

  # Read the LD square matrix
  # Note: adjust 'header' and 'check.names' based on the  file format
  snplist_file <- sub("\\.ld$", ".snplist", ld_file)
  snp_names <- readLines(snplist_file)
  
  # Keep only the first name if multiple are present (separated by ;)
  snp_names <- sub(";.*", "", snp_names)
  
  R_raw <- as.matrix(read.table(ld_file, header = FALSE))
  sum(is.na(R_raw))
  rownames(R_raw) <- colnames(R_raw) <- snp_names
  
  ######### map_file is to map snp id, rsXXXX to coordinate chr:pos since gwas uses coordinate 
  map_file     <- sub("\\.ld$", ".id_map", ld_file)
  #  Read the ID Map (rsID to chr:pos)
  id_lookup <- read.table(map_file, header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  colnames(id_lookup) <- c("rsid", "coords")
  
  # Translate rsIDs to chr:pos based on the map, some are not matched, leading to NA's 
  matched_indices <- match(snp_names, id_lookup$rsid)
  chr_pos_names   <- id_lookup$coords[matched_indices]
  
  # Assign the new chr:pos names to LD matrix
  rownames(R_raw) <- colnames(R_raw) <- chr_pos_names
  snps_in_ld <- colnames(R_raw)
  
  ############################
  # Align GWAS and LD
  ############################
  gwas_locus <- gwas[
      CHR == chr & 
      BP >= (pos - WINDOW) & 
      BP <= (pos + WINDOW)
    ]

  if (nrow(gwas_locus) < 10) {
    warning("Too few GWAS SNPs, skipping ", locus_id)
    next
  }

  # Find overlap between GWAS MarkerName and LD matrix names
  common_snps <- intersect(gwas_locus$MarkerName, snps_in_ld)
  common_snps[1:10]

  if (length(common_snps) < 10) {
    warning("Too few overlapping SNPs, skipping ", locus_id)
    next
  }

  # Subset and Reorder both to match exactly
  # 1. Subset GWAS and set key for fast indexing
  gwas_locus <- gwas_locus[MarkerName %in% common_snps]
  setkey(gwas_locus, MarkerName)
  
  # 2. Subset and Reorder Matrix
  R <- R_raw[common_snps, common_snps]
  na_snps <- colSums(is.na(R)) > 0
# Remove them
  R <- R[!na_snps, !na_snps]


  # 3. Final alignment: Ensure GWAS rows are in the exact same order as R columns
  gwas_locus <- gwas_locus[colnames(R)]

  # Safety check
  stopifnot(identical(gwas_locus$MarkerName, colnames(R)))

  ############### run susieR ###########
  z <- gwas_locus$Effect / gwas_locus$StdErr 

  fit <- susie_rss(
    z = z,
    R = R,
    n = 26533,
    L = L_MAX, 
    estimate_prior_method = "EM",
    estimate_residual_variance = FALSE
  )

  susie_results[[i]] <- list(
      locus = paste0("chr", chr, ":", pos),
      fit   = fit,
      gwas  = gwas_locus
  )
}


#saveRDS(susie_results, file=file.path(root, "../data_samples/dfss_summary_susie_results.RDS"))
```

```{r, message=F, warning=F}
susie_results=readRDS(file.path(root, "../data_samples/dfss_summary_susie_results.RDS"))

summary_list <- list()

for (i in seq_along(susie_results)) {
  res <- susie_results[[i]]
  if (is.null(res)) next
  
  fit <- res$fit
  gwas_data <- res$gwas
  
  # Check if any credible sets were found
  if (!is.null(fit$sets$cs)) {
    
    # 1. Get the indices of SNPs in credible sets
    # fit$sets$cs is a list where each element is a vector of SNP indices
    for (cs_id in names(fit$sets$cs)) {
      snp_idx <- fit$sets$cs[[cs_id]]
      
      # 2. Extract details for these specific SNPs
      cs_snps <- gwas_data[snp_idx, ]
      cs_snps$PIP <- fit$pip[snp_idx]
      cs_snps$CS <- cs_id
      cs_snps$Locus <- res$locus
      cs_snps$Purity <- fit$sets$purity[cs_id, "min.abs.corr"] # Quality check
      
      summary_list[[length(summary_list) + 1]] <- cs_snps
    }
  }
}

# Combine all loci into one data frame
final_summary <- bind_rows(summary_list)

final_summary %>% 
  arrange(desc(PIP)) %>% 
  select(Locus, MarkerName, CS, PIP, Purity, everything()) %>% 
  datatable(extensions = 'Buttons',
          caption = "", 
            options = list(dom = 'Blfrtip',
                           buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf', 'print'),
                           lengthMenu = list(c(10,25,50,-1),
                                             c(10,25,50,"All"))))
```


```{r, echo=FALSE, message=F, warning=F, eval=F}
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
plot_susie_locus <- function(res_list, index) {
  # 1. Extract and check data
  locus_data <- res_list[[index]]
  if (is.null(locus_data)) return(message("Locus index is empty."))
  
  df <- as.data.frame(locus_data$gwas)
  df$PIP <- locus_data$fit$pip
  
  # 2. Identify the P-value column
  # We'll look for common names. Adjust "P" if your column has a unique name.
  p_col <- grep("^(P|PVALUE|P.VALUE|p|pvalue)$", colnames(df), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)
  
  if (length(p_col) == 0) {
    stop("Could not find a P-value column. Available columns are: ", paste(colnames(df), collapse=", "))
  }
  
  # Rename for the plot logic
  df$pval_for_plot <- df[[p_col[1]]]

  # 3. Identify SNPs in Credible Sets
  df$InCS <- "No"
  if (!is.null(locus_data$fit$sets$cs)) {
    for (cs_name in names(locus_data$fit$sets$cs)) {
      idx <- locus_data$fit$sets$cs[[cs_name]]
      df$InCS[idx] <- cs_name
    }
  }

  # 4. GWAS Plot (-log10 P-value)
  p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = BP / 1e6, y = -log10(pval_for_plot), color = InCS)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    # Use a color scale that expands based on number of CS found
    scale_color_manual(values = c("No" = "grey30", 
                                 "L1" = "firebrick1", 
                                 "L2" = "dodgerblue", 
                                 "L3" = "forestgreen",
                                 "L4" = "purple",
                                 "L5" = "orange")) +
    labs(title = paste("Locus:", locus_data$locus), 
         y = expression(-log[10](P)), 
         x = "") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid = element_blank())

  # 5. SuSiE PIP Plot
  p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = BP / 1e6, y = PIP, color = InCS)) +
    geom_point(size = 1.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("No" = "grey30", 
                                 "L1" = "firebrick1", 
                                 "L2" = "dodgerblue", 
                                 "L3" = "forestgreen",
                                 "L4" = "purple",
                                 "L5" = "orange")) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.95, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(y = "SuSiE PIP", x = "Position (Mb)") +
    ylim(0, 1.05) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

  # Stack the plots
  grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 1)
}

# Run the plot
plot_susie_locus(susie_results, 10)
```



[LD matrix inconsistency issue](https://github.com/stephenslab/susieR/issues/148)
